Scenario.title: Denial of Service Attack via Spearphishing
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A basic spearphishing campaign targets employees within transportation organizations, leveraging vulnerable web services to compromise systems and ultimately disrupt operations through denial-of-service attacks.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: To disrupt operational services and potentially extort stakeholders for financial gain or strategic interference.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, File Creation (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker conducts spearphishing by crafting deceptive emails that appear to originate from trusted sources within the transportation sector, such as regulatory agencies or logistics providers. These emails target specific employees and may contain links or attachments that exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing assets, enabling unauthorized access through manipulated web protocols.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Following initial access via phishing, the attacker executes a malicious script embedded in a compromised file delivered through the previous stage. This script exploits weaknesses in software integrity, allowing the attacker to run unauthorized code on the victim's system and establish persistence or prepare for further actions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker leverages the compromised execution to launch a denial-of-service attack by flooding network traffic with malformed or excessive packets directed at key servers and applications in transportation systems. This overwhelms the hardware, preventing legitimate users from accessing services and causing operational disruptions like service outages or delays.

Scenario.summary: This attack scenario demonstrates a simple chain where spearphishing gains initial access, followed by script execution to disable network components, impacting transportation operations.
